workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Working on Azure Ridge Partners's migration architecture. Got the support ticket -- help me build the narrative.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-11 14:08 EST
Subject: migration architecture - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with migration architecture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
Date: 2024-02-24 16:41 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Azure Ridge Partners migration architecture

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-21 13:45 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our CFO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Azure Ridge Partners: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-C trap (Invents prior commitments not in thread); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-C failure mode (Invents prior commitments not in thread)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-C -- Invents prior commitments not in thread
